title: Simola Hillclimb  Entry applications deadline looms for 2026 Simola Hillclimb
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS – It’s just two weeks to go until the window for entry applications for the 16th edition of the Simola Hillclimb closes, and there’s already been fantastic interest from aspiring drivers and teams to compete in this year’s event.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS – It’s just two weeks to go until the window for entry applications for the 16th edition of the Simola Hillclimb closes, and there’s already been fantastic interest from aspiring drivers and teams to compete in this year’s event."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"As South Africa’s premier hillclimb and the leading motoring and motorsport lifestyle event, it’s no surprise that the Simola Hillclimb attracts the top competitors from around the country, and some of the biggest names in international motorsport too."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We have received an impressive number and calibre of entry applications thus far, and are delighted with the response for both Classic Car Friday and King of the Hill,” says Leon Smith, Simola Hillclimb sporting director. “With the 31 January deadline just two weeks away, we encourage all aspiring competitors to submit their applications as soon as possible.","html":"“We have received an impressive number and calibre of entry applications thus far, and are delighted with the response for both Classic Car Friday and King of the Hill,” says Leon Smith, Simola Hillclimb sporting director. “With the 31 January deadline just two weeks away, we encourage all aspiring competitors to submit their applications as soon as possible."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Our goal every year is to deliver an exciting line-up of cars and drivers to keep the field competitive and the spectators enthralled,” Smith says. “The level of local and international interest continues to grow each year, and we look forward to raising the bar even higher in 2026.”","html":"“Our goal every year is to deliver an exciting line-up of cars and drivers to keep the field competitive and the spectators enthralled,” Smith says. “The level of local and international interest continues to grow each year, and we look forward to raising the bar even higher in 2026.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The 16th edition of the Simola Hillclimb takes place from 30 April to 3 May. The opening day on 30 April kicks off the proceedings with scrutineering for the Classic Car Friday contenders, followed by the spectacular parade along the Knysna Waterfront – both of which are open to the public with free access.","html":"The 16th edition of the Simola Hillclimb takes place from 30 April to 3 May. The opening day on 30 April kicks off the proceedings with scrutineering for the Classic Car Friday contenders, followed by the spectacular parade along the Knysna Waterfront – both of which are open to the public with free access."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Classic Car Friday on 1 May allows a total of 65 competitors spread across 10 classes – from the Golden Oldies of a century ago to many of the legendary cars that ruled our streets and racing circuits over the decades that followed, all the way up to the early 2000s.","html":"Classic Car Friday on 1 May allows a total of 65 competitors spread across 10 classes – from the Golden Oldies of a century ago to many of the legendary cars that ruled our streets and racing circuits over the decades that followed, all the way up to the early 2000s."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"King of the Hill runs over two days on Saturday 2 May and Sunday 3 May. It comprises 84 entries featuring standard road cars, dream-worthy supercars, highly tuned road-legal machines, wild modified saloon racing cars and thoroughbred single-seaters and sports prototypes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"From 1 February, the submitted entries will be reviewed by the Simola Hillclimb organisers. Accepted drivers will receive their invitations to participate by 14 February.","html":"From 1 February, the submitted entries will be reviewed by the Simola Hillclimb organisers. Accepted drivers will receive their invitations to participate by 14 February."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The 16th edition of the Simola Hillclimb takes place from 30 April to 3 May.","html":"The 16th edition of the Simola Hillclimb takes place from 30 April to 3 May."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For more information, including applications for entry, visit the website: www.simolahillclimb.com","html":"For more information, including applications for entry, visit the website: www.simolahillclimb.com"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
